core_mechanism: finite-size scaling of observables near a continuous critical point, in which the leading power-law singularities acquire multiplicative and additive logarithmic corrections characteristic of a marginal (borderline) case
brief_reason: The abstract analyzes scaling and finite-size-scaling behavior at and off criticality and reports new multiplicative and (partly universal) additive logarithmic corrections to the power-law singularities of susceptibility and specific heat, verified by high-precision Monte Carlo.
